title: SOE Rhythm Quest: The Summer Stretch Workbook
subtitle: The Summer Stretch

A.title: Word Detectives: Greetings
A.img_alt: Children waving hello in Harmonia village
A.instructions: Look at the picture. Find 3 greeting words. Trace each one, then write it yourself below.
A.tip: Amara says: When you learn a new greeting, practice it with your whole face — smile big!

B.title: Numeria Number Hunt
B.img_alt: Number tiles and counting beads in Numeria
B.instructions: Count the objects in the picture. Write the total in each box. Circle the biggest number.
B.tip: Kwame says: Use your finger to count carefully. Slow and steady wins the math race!

C.title: Terrasol Garden Secrets
C.img_alt: Seeds sprouting in Terrasol garden
C.phonetic: SEED | ROOT | SPROUT | LEEF | SUN
C.instructions: Draw a seed under the soil and a sprout above it. Label 3 parts: seed, root, sprout.
C.tip: Silas says: Every giant tree started as a tiny seed — just like every big reader started with one word!

D.title: Vitalis Wake-Up Move
D.img_alt: Children stretching in Vitalis
D.instructions: Stand up! Do 5 arm circles forward, 5 backward. Take 3 deep breaths. Shake out your hands. You're ready to learn!
D.tip: Nerissa says: Your brain learns better when your body moves first!

E.title: Aquaria Map Adventure
E.img_alt: Mountains, rivers, and plains on an Aquaria map
E.phonetic: MOWN-tin | RIV-er | PLAYN | oh-SHUN
E.instructions: Look at the landform picture. Can you find a mountain? A river? Draw your own simple map with 2 landforms and label them.
E.tip: Ronan says: Maps are stories about places. Every map tells you something new!
E.subject: Geography

F.reflection_prompt: What was your favorite activity today? Draw a star next to it and write one word that describes how you feel right now.

G.title: Pencil Power: Grip & Posture
G.img_alt: Children writing at desks in Harmonia
G.instructions: Hold your pencil with thumb and pointer finger, resting on your middle finger. Sit tall, feet flat. Trace the dotted letters A, B, C three times each. Circle your best one!
G.tip: Amara says: A strong pencil grip is the first step to confident writing. Your hand is your most powerful tool!

A.title: Daily Routine Words
A.img_alt: Morning routines in Harmonia homes
A.instructions: Put these daily routine words in order: eat, sleep, wake, brush teeth, go to school. Write numbers 1–5 next to them.
A.tip: Amara says: Words about your day are the easiest to remember — you live them!

B.title: What Shape Is That?
B.img_alt: Geometric shapes in Numeria marketplace
B.instructions: Find 3 shapes in the picture. Draw each one in a box. Write how many sides each shape has.
B.tip: Kwame says: Look around the room. How many rectangles can you spot right now?

C.title: Wild Animal Watch
C.img_alt: Wild animals in the Terrasol forest
C.phonetic: LY-on | el-uh-FUNT | GI-raff | ZE-bruh
C.instructions: Pick 2 animals from the picture. Draw a simple habitat for each one. Write one word that describes each animal.
C.tip: Vesta says: Scientists who study animals are called zoologists. You're a junior zoologist today!

D.title: Vitalis Healthy Habits Check
D.img_alt: Good hygiene habits in Vitalis
D.instructions: Check off each habit you did today: ☐ Brush teeth ☐ Wash hands ☐ Drink water ☐ Sleep well ☐ Move body. How many checked?
D.tip: Octavia says: 5 out of 5 makes you a Vitalis Champion!

E.title: Luminosity: Rights & Duties
E.img_alt: Community members working together in Luminosity
E.phonetic: RITE | doo-TEE | re-SPON-sih-BIL-ih-tee
E.instructions: A right is something you have. A duty is something you do. Write 1 right and 1 duty you have at home. Share with someone!
E.tip: Felix says: Good citizens know their rights AND their responsibilities.
E.subject: Civics

F.reflection_prompt: Draw your face right now. Are you happy, tired, focused, or excited? Write the word under your drawing.

G.title: Tall Letters: L, T, H, K
G.img_alt: Tall letter practice in Harmonia school
G.instructions: Tall letters touch the top line! Practice L, T, H, K — trace 3, then write 3 on your own. Write one word starting with each letter. Keep them tall and proud!
G.tip: Kwame says: Tall letters stand with confidence — just like you when you know the answer!

A.title: Feelings Words Explore
A.img_alt: Children showing different emotions in Harmonia
A.instructions: Find 4 emotion words in the picture. Write each one and draw the face that matches.
A.tip: Amara says: Knowing how you feel helps you talk to others. Feelings words are power words!

B.title: Measurement Mission
B.img_alt: Rulers, cups, and scales in a Numeria home
B.instructions: Use your hand as a ruler. Measure 3 objects near you in 'hand lengths'. Write: Object, Measurement, Longer or Shorter than a pencil?
B.tip: Kwame says: Before rulers existed, people measured with body parts. You're using ancient math!

C.title: Weather Watchers Report
C.img_alt: Four seasons shown in Terrasol landscape
C.phonetic: RAIN | SUN-ee | SNOW | WIND | KLOU-dee
C.instructions: Look outside. What is the weather right now? Write: Today's weather is ___. Draw the weather symbol. Circle the matching season.
C.tip: Silas says: Weather scientists are called meteorologists. Report the weather like a pro!

D.title: Vitalis Sports Moves
D.img_alt: Children playing different sports in Vitalis
D.instructions: Do each action for 10 seconds: hop on one foot, march in place, jump with both feet, spin slowly. Rest and breathe.
D.tip: Nerissa says: Every time you move, you make your heart stronger. Go team!

E.title: Aquaria: Ocean Life
E.img_alt: Colorful sea creatures in the Aquaria ocean
E.phonetic: oh-SHUN | FISH | KOR-ul | WAIV | DEAP
E.instructions: Find 3 ocean creatures in the picture. Draw your favorite in the box below. Write its name and one amazing fact about it.
E.tip: Selene says: The ocean covers 71% of Earth. It's the biggest habitat on the planet!
E.subject: Geography

F.reflection_prompt: What did you learn today that surprised you? Write one sentence and add 3 stars if you're proud of your work.

G.title: Round Letters: O, C, G, Q
G.img_alt: Round letter shapes in Harmonia art room
G.instructions: Round letters start like a clock — at 2 o'clock, curve left! Practice O, C, G, Q. Trace 3, write 3. Which round letter is hardest? Practice that one 5 more times.
G.tip: Amara says: Round letters flow like music — smooth and connected. Let your hand dance!

A.title: Classroom Word Wall
A.img_alt: A colorful classroom in Harmonia
A.instructions: Find 5 classroom objects in the picture. Write each word. Then write the first letter of each word in a column — do they spell anything?
A.tip: Amara says: Your classroom is your first learning land. Know its vocabulary!

B.title: Clock Detectives
B.img_alt: Analog and digital clocks in Numeria
B.instructions: Draw these times on the blank clock faces: 3:00, 7:30, 12:00. What time do you wake up? Draw that too!
B.tip: Kwame says: Reading a clock is a superpower. You can always know where you are in the day!

C.title: Recycling Rangers
C.img_alt: Recycling bins and sorting in Terrasol
C.phonetic: re-SY-kul | COM-post | RE-DOOSE | re-YOOZ
C.instructions: Sort these items: paper, banana peel, plastic bottle, cardboard, apple core. Draw them in the correct bin: Recycle / Compost / Trash.
C.tip: Vesta says: Every item you recycle saves energy. Small acts = big impact!

D.title: Vitalis Body Knowledge
D.img_alt: Labeled human body diagram in Vitalis
D.instructions: Point to your heart, lungs, and brain on your own body. Jump 10 times. Press your hand to your chest — feel your heart beating faster?
D.tip: Octavia says: Your heart beats about 100,000 times a day. Thank your heart!

E.title: Luminosity: Community Helpers
E.img_alt: Police officers, firefighters, and doctors serving in Luminosity
E.phonetic: DOKT-er | FYR-fyt-er | TEE-cher | NER-s | PO-leece
E.instructions: Write the name of 2 community helpers you know personally. Draw one of them. What superpower do they have?
E.tip: Felix says: Community helpers make our neighborhood safe and strong. Who's your hero?
E.subject: Civics

F.reflection_prompt: Which community helper would you like to be someday? Write their job title and one reason why.

G.title: Short Letters: a, e, i, o, u
G.img_alt: Lowercase vowels on a Harmonia chalkboard
G.instructions: Lowercase vowels sit between the middle and bottom lines. Write each vowel 5 times. Then write 5 words that START with a vowel. Keep them small and neat!
G.tip: Kwame says: Vowels are the heartbeat of every word. Master them and your writing sings!

A.title: My Info Book Page
A.img_alt: A personal ID card in Harmonia
A.instructions: Fill in your personal info page: Name, Age, Favorite Color, Favorite Food, Best Friend, Dream Job. Decorate the border!
A.tip: Amara says: Knowing how to write your own information is an important life skill!

B.title: Chart Masters
B.img_alt: Bar graphs and pie charts in Numeria
B.instructions: Ask 3 family members: Cats or dogs? Draw a tally chart with your results. Then draw a simple bar graph to show the winner!
B.tip: Kwame says: Data is just information organized so we can understand it better!

C.title: Scientific Method Steps
C.img_alt: Science steps diagram in Celestia
C.phonetic: HY-poth-uh-sis | ob-SER-vay-shun | EK-sper-ih-ment | CON-kloo-zhun
C.instructions: Put these steps in order: Observe, Question, Hypothesize, Test, Conclude. Write 1-5 next to each. What question would you love to test?
C.tip: Elias says: Every great scientist asks 'What if?' What's your favorite 'What if?' question?

D.title: Vitalis Nutrition Plate
D.img_alt: A healthy plate with food groups in Vitalis
D.instructions: Draw a plate divided in half. Fill half with fruits and vegetables. Add a grain and a protein. Draw your dream healthy meal!
D.tip: Nerissa says: Colorful plates are healthy plates. Eat the rainbow!

E.title: Celestia Week 1 Review
E.img_alt: A reflection scroll in Celestia library
E.phonetic: re-VIEW | re-MEM-ber | LERND | PROUD | NEXT
E.instructions: Look back at your work from Days 1–4. Circle your best page. Write 3 things you learned this week. Give yourself a Quest Star ⭐!
E.tip: Ezra says: Reviewing what you learned makes it stick in your memory forever!
E.subject: Review & Reflection

F.reflection_prompt: Write 3 words that describe your Week 1 journey. Color a star for each activity you completed this week!

G.title: My Name in Perfect Print
G.img_alt: Children writing their names proudly in Harmonia
G.instructions: Write your FULL NAME in your best handwriting — 5 times! Capital first letter, lowercase rest. Decorate your favorite version with a border. This is YOUR signature style!
G.tip: Amara says: Your name is the most important word you'll ever write. Make it beautiful — it represents YOU!

A.title: Family Words
A.img_alt: Families in Harmonia village square
A.instructions: Write the names of 5 family members or close friends. Draw one family scene below with labels.
A.tip: Amara says: Every family looks different — all families are special!

B.title: Calendar Math
B.img_alt: A large calendar with numbers in Numeria
B.instructions: Write today's full date. How many days until your next birthday? Which month has the most letters?
B.tip: Kwame says: Calendars are time maps. Always know where you are in the year!

C.title: Inside Your Home
C.img_alt: Rooms inside a cozy Terrasol home
C.phonetic: KITCH-en | BED-room | BATH-room | LIV-ing | FLOOR
C.instructions: Draw your home's floor plan from memory. Label: kitchen, bedroom, bathroom, living room. Add your favorite spot with a star!
C.tip: Silas says: Your home is your base camp for all adventures!

D.title: Wake-Up Stretch Routine
D.img_alt: Morning stretch sequence
D.instructions: Do the Harmony Morning Flow: Reach up 5×, touch toes 5×, twist left-right 5×, take 5 deep breaths. Feel calm and ready!
D.tip: Octavia says: A morning body warm-up = a morning brain warm-up!

E.title: Aquaria: Map Reading
E.img_alt: A treasure map in the Aquaria jungle
E.phonetic: MAP | KOM-pass | NORTH | EAST | WEST | SOUTH
E.instructions: Draw a compass rose with N, S, E, W. Then draw a simple map of your bedroom with a compass rose in the corner.
E.tip: Ronan says: Every explorer needs to know: which way is North?
E.subject: Geography

F.reflection_prompt: Who is one person in your family or community you are grateful for? Write their name and one reason.

G.title: Finger Spaces Matter
G.img_alt: Words with proper spacing in Harmonia notebook
G.instructions: Use your pointer finger to make spaces between words! Write: 'I love my family' with perfect finger spaces. Now write 3 sentences about your family with clear spaces.
G.tip: Amara says: Finger spaces make your writing readable. If someone can read it easily, you've done it right!

A.title: Pets at Home
A.img_alt: Pets and owners in Harmonia homes
A.instructions: Write the pet names from the picture. Do you have a pet? Draw and label it. If not, draw your dream pet!
A.tip: Kwame says: Learning names of animals in English AND your home language doubles your vocabulary!

B.title: Pattern Power
B.img_alt: Repeating patterns in Numeria tiles
B.instructions: Find the pattern rule for each row in the picture. Draw the next 2 shapes. Create your own A-B-C pattern using colors or shapes!
B.tip: Kwame says: Math is the science of patterns. Patterns are everywhere!

C.title: Terrasol Pets Science
C.img_alt: Animals as pets in Terrasol setting
C.phonetic: MAM-ul | BIRD | REP-tile | AM-fib-ee-an
C.instructions: Pick one pet from the picture. Write: What it eats, where it sleeps, and one fun fact. Use the picture for clues!
C.tip: Vesta says: Caring for pets teaches responsibility and empathy!

D.title: Vitalis Cool-Down Flow
D.img_alt: Healthy bedtime routine in Vitalis
D.instructions: Slow body, calm mind: Roll your shoulders back 3×. Nod your head slowly 3×. Breathe in for 4 counts, out for 4. Wiggle fingers and rest.
D.tip: Octavia says: Calm bodies learn the most. You've earned this rest!

E.title: Luminosity: Government Basics
E.img_alt: Leaders and government buildings in Luminosity
E.phonetic: GOV-ern-ment | LEED-er | LAW | VOT | COM-yoo-nih-tee
E.instructions: Name 2 leaders in your community (school, city, or country). Write: Name, Job Title, One way they help people.
E.tip: Felix says: Government exists to help communities work well together!
E.subject: Civics

F.reflection_prompt: What pattern did you notice in your day today? (e.g., wake up → eat → learn → play → sleep). Draw or write your daily pattern.

G.title: Letter Pairs: th, sh, ch
G.img_alt: Digraph letter pairs on a Harmonia poster
G.instructions: Some letters work in pairs! Write th, sh, ch 5 times each. Now write 3 words for each pair (the, she, chin). Underline the letter pairs in each word.
G.tip: Kwame says: Letter pairs are best friends that make new sounds together. Listen for them everywhere!

A.title: Colors & Patterns Words
A.img_alt: Colorful patterns in Harmonia art class
A.phonetic: RED | BLOO | GRĪIN | YEL-oh | PUR-pul | or-INJ
A.instructions: Write 6 color words from the picture. Then write each color in its own color (use crayons or pencils if you have them!)
A.tip: Amara says: Colors have names in every language. Learn the rainbow in 2 languages!

B.title: Fractions Fun
B.img_alt: Fractions shown with shapes and food
B.instructions: Fold a piece of paper in half. That's 1/2! Fold again — that's 1/4! Draw and label: 1 whole, 1/2, 1/4 of a circle.
B.tip: Kwame says: Cutting a pizza = fractions! You already know more math than you think!

C.title: Insects & Bugs Field Guide
C.img_alt: Colorful insects illustrated in Terrasol meadow
C.phonetic: IN-sekt | BUT-er-fly | BEE | ANT | CRICK-et | LEG
C.instructions: Find an insect in the picture. Write: name, number of legs, wings (yes/no), where it lives. Compare with a second insect!
C.tip: Silas says: All insects have 6 legs. Use that to tell bugs from spiders!

D.title: Vitalis Jump Circuit
D.img_alt: Children doing jumping exercises in Vitalis
D.instructions: Complete the circuit: 10 jumping jacks, 5 hops on each foot, 10 skips (alternate feet). Rest. Repeat if you have energy!
D.tip: Nerissa says: Circuits build heart strength AND coordination. Go go go!

E.title: Aquaria: Directions
E.img_alt: Street signs and direction arrows in Aquaria city
E.phonetic: LEST | RITE | STRATE | TER-n | FOR-werd | BACK
E.instructions: Give directions from your room to the kitchen using: left, right, straight, turn. Draw the route like a top-view map.
E.tip: Selene says: Knowing directions keeps you from getting lost. Be your own map!
E.subject: Geography

F.reflection_prompt: If you could paint a mural of your favorite land, which one would you choose? Sketch a tiny version here.

G.title: Sizing Matters: Big & Small
G.img_alt: Letters in different sizes on colorful paper
G.instructions: Write the alphabet — keeping all tall letters the SAME height and all short letters the SAME height. Consistency is the secret to beautiful handwriting!
G.tip: Amara says: Consistent letter sizes make your writing look professional. You're becoming a penmanship pro!

A.title: Manners Matter
A.img_alt: Children being polite in Harmonia market
A.instructions: Write 5 polite phrases from the picture. Practice saying each one aloud. Write one sentence using 2 of these phrases.
A.tip: Amara says: Kind words open every door. Practice them today!

B.title: Money Makes Sense
B.img_alt: Coins and bills in Numeria market stalls
B.phonetic: COIN | BIL | CENT | DOL-er | PRICE | CHANJE
B.instructions: If you have $1.00 and a candy costs $0.35, how much change do you get? Draw the coins you'd receive. Try with $0.75 too!
B.tip: Kwame says: Being good with money starts with learning to count it!

C.title: Fruits & Vegetables Lab
C.img_alt: Colorful produce in a Terrasol farmer's market
C.phonetic: FROOT | VEJ-ih-tuh-bul | SEED | SKIN | JOO-see
C.instructions: Sort these into Fruit or Vegetable: apple, carrot, banana, broccoli, mango, spinach. How do you know which is which? Write your rule.
C.tip: Vesta says: A fruit has seeds inside. A vegetable is a stem, leaf, or root. Check your rule!

D.title: Vitalis Mindful Moment
D.img_alt: Children breathing and being mindful in Vitalis
D.instructions: Sit up straight. Close your eyes. Breathe in slowly — 4 counts. Hold — 2 counts. Out slowly — 6 counts. Do this 3 times. How do you feel?
D.tip: Octavia says: 3 mindful breaths can change your whole mood. Science says so!

E.title: Luminosity: Types of Workers
E.img_alt: Many occupations illustrated in Luminosity city
E.phonetic: WER-ker | JOB | OC-yoo-PAY-shun | SKILL | EARN
E.instructions: Pick 2 workers from the picture. Write: Job Name, What they do, What skills they need. Which job would you choose?
E.tip: Athena says: Every job is important. Community works because people share responsibilities!
E.subject: Civics

F.reflection_prompt: What is one way you showed good manners today? Write it down. Good manners are a superpower!

G.title: Descenders: g, j, p, q, y
G.img_alt: Letters that go below the line in Harmonia
G.instructions: Some letters have tails that dip BELOW the line! Practice g, j, p, q, y — trace 3, write 5. Write 5 words with descenders. Keep those tails neat!
G.tip: Kwame says: Descenders are letters with roots — they reach down deep, just like strong learners!

A.title: Hobby & Hobbies Page
A.img_alt: Children enjoying hobbies in Harmonia park
A.phonetic: HOB-ee | PLAY | DRAW | READ | DAN-s | SPORT
A.instructions: Write 5 hobbies from the picture. Circle your favorite. Write 3 sentences: 'I love to ___ because ___.'
A.tip: Kwame says: Hobbies feed your soul and grow your skills. Protect your hobbies!

B.title: Estimation Challenge
B.img_alt: Estimation jars and comparison problems
B.instructions: Grab a handful of small objects (coins, beans, buttons). Estimate first, then count. Write: My estimate ___, Actual count ___, Difference ___.
B.tip: Kwame says: Estimation is making a smart guess. The best estimators count the most carefully!

C.title: Rocks & Minerals Study
C.img_alt: Colorful rocks and crystals in Terrasol caves
C.phonetic: ROK | MIN-er-ul | KRIS-tul | SMOOTH | ROUGH | HARD
C.instructions: Find 2 rocks near you (outside or in a picture). Describe each: Color, Texture (smooth/rough), Weight (heavy/light), Shiny or dull.
C.tip: Silas says: Every rock tells the story of where it's been. Rocks are Earth's diary!

D.title: Vitalis Rest & Recovery
D.img_alt: Healthy sleep habits and rest in Vitalis
D.instructions: How many hours did you sleep last night? Kids need 9-11 hours! Write your bedtime routine: 3 steps that help you sleep well.
D.tip: Nerissa says: Sleep = brain recharge. Your brain sorts and saves memories while you sleep!

E.title: Celestia Week 2 Review
E.img_alt: Stars and reflection tools in Celestia
E.phonetic: re-FLEKT | re-VIEW | GROW | LERND | NEXT-WEEK
E.instructions: Draw your Week 2 highlight. Write: 1 thing I learned, 1 thing I want to learn more about, 1 way I grew as a learner this week.
E.tip: Ezra says: Reflection turns experience into wisdom. Future-you thanks present-you!
E.subject: Review & Reflection

F.reflection_prompt: Color the Quest Map star for Week 2. You earned it! Write one word that describes your summer quest so far.

G.title: Copying a Sentence Perfectly
G.img_alt: Perfect sentence copying in Harmonia study hall
G.instructions: Copy this sentence in your BEST writing: 'Every great journey begins with a single step.' Compare yours to the original — spacing, sizing, neatness. Rate yourself 1-5!
G.tip: Amara says: Careful copying trains your hand and eye to work together. Precision builds confidence!

A.title: The Neighborhood
A.img_alt: Streets, parks and shops in Harmonia neighborhood
A.instructions: Write 6 neighborhood places from the picture. Which one do you visit most? Draw your street and label 3 places.
A.tip: Amara says: Your neighborhood is your first world. Know every part of it!

B.title: Problem Solving Logic
B.img_alt: Logic puzzles in Numeria classroom
B.instructions: Read: There are 12 apples. 4 children each want the same amount. How many does each child get? Show your work in 3 steps!
B.tip: Kwame says: Every math problem is a puzzle waiting to be solved step by step!

C.title: The Environment Project
C.img_alt: Clean rivers and forests in Terrasol
C.phonetic: en-VY-ron-ment | PO-loo-shun | PRO-tekt | NA-tur | KLEEN
C.instructions: Draw a before/after: one side shows a polluted environment, the other shows a clean one. Write 2 things YOU can do to help.
C.tip: Vesta says: The environment is everyone's home. Protect what you love!

D.title: Vitalis Team Fitness
D.img_alt: Team sports and cooperation in Vitalis
D.instructions: Play mirror game: stand facing a partner or mirror. One person leads slow movements. Switch! Leaders: be creative. Followers: be precise!
D.tip: Nerissa says: Sports teach us to work together AND listen carefully!

E.title: Aquaria: Community Places
E.img_alt: Community buildings from a bird's eye view in Aquaria
E.phonetic: SKOOL | HOS-pih-tul | LIB-ruh-ree | POST-of-is | PARK
E.instructions: Write 4 community places. Write why each one is important. Which one would be hardest to live without? Defend your answer!
E.tip: Ronan says: Community places are shared treasure. Take care of them!
E.subject: Geography

F.reflection_prompt: What makes a good neighbor? Write 3 qualities. Are you that kind of neighbor? Circle: Yes / Working on it / Not yet — then make a plan!

G.title: Writing Sight Words Beautifully
G.img_alt: Sight words displayed beautifully in Harmonia
G.instructions: Write these 10 sight words in your best handwriting: the, and, is, was, are, have, they, from, with, said. Circle the 3 you wrote best. Practice the 3 hardest again.
G.tip: Amara says: When sight words flow from your hand automatically, your brain is free to think bigger thoughts!

A.title: Communication Tech
A.img_alt: Phones, computers and letters in Harmonia
A.instructions: List 5 ways people communicate from the picture. Old or New? Mark each O (old) or N (new). Which way do you use most?
A.tip: Amara says: Communication connects us to the world. Choose your words carefully!

B.title: The Grocery Store Math
B.img_alt: Shopping at a busy Numeria market
B.instructions: Make a grocery list for a pretend party. Write 5 items with prices. Calculate the total. You have $10 — can you afford it all?
B.tip: Kwame says: Shopping on a budget is real math. You're a financial superstar!

C.title: Farm to Table Journey
C.img_alt: Crops being harvested and brought to market
C.phonetic: FARM | HAR-vest | TRANS-port | STORE | COOK | EET
C.instructions: Draw the journey of a tomato: seed → plant → harvest → store → your plate! Label each step with an action word.
C.tip: Silas says: Understanding where food comes from makes every meal more meaningful!

D.title: Vitalis First Aid Basics
D.img_alt: First aid kit and basic injury care in Vitalis
D.instructions: Write what you should do for: a small cut, a bruise, a burn. Write STOP, LOOK, THINK before each answer. Ask a adult to check!
D.tip: Octavia says: Knowing first aid is knowing how to help. Be a helper!

E.title: Luminosity: Occupations
E.img_alt: Workers in different careers in Luminosity
E.phonetic: OK-yoo-PAY-shun | CAREER | SKILL | TRAIN | EARN
E.instructions: Pick your dream career from the picture. Write: Job name, 3 skills needed, 1 reason you'd love it. Interview yourself in 3 Q&As!
E.tip: Felix says: Exploring career options now helps you dream bigger later!
E.subject: Civics

F.reflection_prompt: If you could help your community in ANY way, what would you do? Write your community superpower!

G.title: Speed vs. Neatness Balance
G.img_alt: Fast and neat writing comparison in Harmonia
G.instructions: Write 'The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog' (it has every letter!) slowly and beautifully. Then write it fast. Compare! Find YOUR best speed-neatness balance.
G.tip: Kwame says: The goal isn't slow OR fast — it's finding YOUR rhythm where speed meets beauty!

A.title: Music Instruments
A.img_alt: Musical instruments displayed in Harmonia stage
A.instructions: Write 5 instrument names. Sort them: String, Wind, or Percussion. Which one sounds like a heartbeat? Which like the wind?
A.tip: Amara says: Music is the universal language. It needs no translation!

B.title: Distance & Speed
B.img_alt: Race tracks and speed charts in Numeria
B.phonetic: DIS-tans | SPEED | FAST | SLOW | MILE | KIL-oh-ME-ter
B.instructions: If you walk 3 steps per second, how many steps in 10 seconds? 30 seconds? 1 minute? Now time yourself walking 20 steps. How fast?
B.tip: Kwame says: Speed = distance ÷ time. You just did physics!

C.title: Trees of the Forest
C.img_alt: Tall trees and forest floor in Terrasol
C.phonetic: TREE | BARK | BRANCH | LEEF | ROOT | SAP
C.instructions: Go outside or look at a tree image. Write 5 tree parts. Draw a tree and label all 5 parts. What animals might live in this tree?
C.tip: Vesta says: One tree can be home to hundreds of creatures. Trees are apartment buildings!

D.title: Vitalis Dance Break
D.img_alt: Children dancing freely in Vitalis studio
D.instructions: Play your favorite song. Dance freely for the whole song! No rules. Move every part of your body. When it ends, breathe and smile.
D.tip: Nerissa says: Dance is exercise disguised as joy. Sneak in the movement!
